With a combination Of the
women and the forces Of union
labor to fight for police matrons
and the Increased wage scale, there
promises to be some stirring times
around the city hall.


This is all due to the action OF
the city council last night. in max
ing two of it's famous flip flops in
one evening. knocking out the raise
in wages and the three police mat
rons that it had ordered the night
tSfore.


The charge of Chief of Police Sui
Nvar. backed up by Mayor Pratt,
that Ile move for police matrons
was inspired by the women sympa-
thizers of the l. w. w., has stirred
Che fighting blood of the women,
and they propose to show before
they get through that the police de-
partment Is not entirely the spot
less organization that Chief Of Po-
lice Sullivan has made out.


They will at the same time try to
show that "the interests" are all
powerful at the city hall, and that
lot of men in public office in Spo-
kane wear the collars of one power
or another.


The Central Labor union has
gone on record for police matrons
and the wage increase. The action
Of the council on the wage increase
has been a direct slap to the organ
lied workers Of the city and move
dictated by the moneyed interests.


The war on the city hall and the
police department has now opened
up right. Before 1t is through.
some things will be brought to light
that will interest the public.


MEETING THIS MORNING.


For two years the Woman's club
has demanded a police matron at
the city hall. and the assertion that
1t was an I. w. w. move has stirred
them to boiling pitch. A confer
ence Of the leaders Of the Womens
club was in session all of this fore
noon in the offices of Mrs. l.. w.
Hutton in the Hutton block and the
women agreed to stand by Mrs.
Stafford, spokesman of the COM
mittee of the Women's club, to the
end. Mrs. Hutton, in the session
